entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The dominant commercial Partner engine; 75/90hp outputs balance economy and load capability for UK trades.
compatibleVariants: S, SE, Professional, Tepee, Crew Van, Panel Van
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging at 60-100k miles on short-journey commercial use
DPF regeneration failure triggering limp mode without active motorway runs
Turbo actuator wear causing boost pressure loss on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6DTED Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Mid-range 90hp DV6 variant; most searched Partner engine code for commercial replacement in the UK.
compatibleVariants: SE, Professional, Tepee, Panel Van, Crew Van
commonFailures: DPF warning lights and forced regeneration cycles on urban commercial patterns
Injector carbon buildup causing rough idle and cold-start difficulty
Turbo oil feed line coking leading to reduced boost on 100k+ mile units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HV Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: High-output 110hp DV6 for laden Professional and Crew Van models; turbo upgrades common in rebuilds.
compatibleVariants: Professional, Crew Van, Panel Van, Allure
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal leaks causing blue smoke and oil consumption
Timing belt fragmentation damage if service intervals missed
EGR cooler cracking on high-mileage commercial units
entries.cta: Get quotes for 9HX Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: BlueHDi evolution with AdBlue/SCR for Euro 6 compliance; updated emission calibration for 2018+ Partners.
commonFailures: AdBlue injector faults triggering emission warning lights
SCR system calibration issues after battery disconnect
Updated belt-in-oil design requires specialist rebuild knowledge
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6FC Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: Successor to DV6; 1.5L BlueHDi with revised architecture for improved efficiency and emissions on Mk3 Partners.
commonFailures: EGR cooler cracking on early production units
AdBlue system sensor faults causing limp mode
Revised belt-in-oil design demands specialist rebuild expertise
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5RC Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.9L Diesel
entries.history: Legacy naturally aspirated diesel for Mk1 Partners; simple, robust and affordable to rebuild for budget commercial use.
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks causing diesel smell and hard starting
Glow plug relay failures affecting cold-weather operation
Oil consumption from piston ring wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for WJY Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: Modern three-cylinder PureTech for Mk3 Partners; belt-in-oil design offers efficiency but requires specialist rebuild attention.
commonFailures: Belt-in-oil degradation causing fragmentation and oil pickup blockage
Timing belt wear requiring specialist inspection at 60k mile intervals
Oil dilution from direct injection on short-journey use
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADT Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: Smooth 1.6L VTi petrol for passenger-oriented Tepee models; less common in commercial Panel Van configurations.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattles on cold start
VANOS solenoid faults affecting variable valve timing
Carbon buildup on intake valves from direct injection
entries.cta: Get quotes for 5FS Peugeot Partner engine replacement

entries.title: 1.4L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 1.4L petrol for early Mk1 Partners; simple SOHC design makes rebuilds affordable but power is limited for laden use.
commonFailures: Coil pack failures causing misfire and rough running
Throttle body carbon buildup affecting idle quality
Oil consumption from piston ring wear on high-mileage units

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot Partner Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot Partner's 1.6L HDi diesel (DV6 series) dominates UK commercial use but faces DPF regeneration issues, EGR clogging and turbo wear on high-mileage vans. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: DPF Regeneration Failure - Why Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Partner S, SE, Professional, Tepee, Crew Van (2008-2018, DV6DTED, 9HV, 9HX)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent active DPF regeneration cycles, causing soot accumulation that triggers warning lights and reduced power.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic reset and stationary regeneration cycle
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix only; fails again if driving pattern unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-vehicle DPF cleaning or replacement with calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores function but doesn't address root cause of clogging

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV6 engine with compatible DPF system, upgraded EGR components
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; addresses multiple wear points with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: If your Partner is used for commercial short-journey work, a rebuilt engine with updated DPF calibration offers better long-term value than repeated cleaning cycles. We recommend comparing quotes before committing to dealer-priced DPF replacement.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DV6DTED engine prices for your Peugeot Partner

problems.h4: EGR Valve Clogging - Loss of Power on Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Partner all 1.6 HDi variants (2008-2024, DV6DTED, 9HV, 9HX, DV6FC)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation valve accumulates carbon deposits from diesel combustion, restricting airflow and triggering fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-vehicle cleaning or blanking plate installation
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective short-term; may affect emissions compliance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished EGR valve with cooler, recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance; cooler may still clog on high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 engine with updated EGR cooler specification, full system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution; eliminates recurrence risk with warranty coverage

problems.recommendation: For commercial Partners covering high annual mileage, investing in a rebuilt engine with revised EGR cooler design prevents repeat failures and minimises business downtime.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Partner 9HV engine replacement with updated EGR components

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure - Boost Loss on High-Mileage Peugeot Partner
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi 90/110, Professional, Crew Van (2010-2018, 9HX, DV6DTED)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed line coking and wastegate actuator wear cause boost pressure loss, blue smoke and reduced performance.

repairOptions.longevity: Addresses symptom only; underlying turbo wear may persist

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or reconditioned turbocharger with oil feed line service
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost; doesn't address other engine wear on high-mileage units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 engine with upgraded turbo specification, new oil feed lines, full calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution; comprehensive warranty for commercial use

problems.recommendation: On a Peugeot Partner worth £3,000-£8,000, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components offers better value than standalone turbo replacement plus risk of secondary failures.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 9HX engine prices for your Peugeot Partner Professional

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Damage Risk on Peugeot Partner DV6
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Partner all 1.6 HDi variants (2008-2018, DV6DTED, 9HV, 9HX)
problems.rootCause: Missed timing belt changes cause belt fragmentation; debris enters sump, blocks oil pickup and destroys bearings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing belt, tensioner, water pump replacement per schedule
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic failure if done on time

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 engine with updated belt kit, new bearings, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable option after belt failure; includes upgraded components and warranty

problems.recommendation: There is no intermediate repair after timing belt failure on the DV6 engine. If your Partner has suffered belt fragmentation, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable path forward.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Partner DV6DTED rebuilt engine with updated timing kit

problems.h4: Fuel Injector Failure - Misfire and Hard Starting on Peugeot Partner HDi
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot Partner all 1.6 HDi variants (2008-2024, all DV6 codes)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injector nozzles wear or carbon-fouled injectors cause uneven fuel delivery, misfires and difficult cold starts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-vehicle cleaning and recoding to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary improvement; worn injectors will fail again

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or refurbished injectors with calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance; other injectors may fail soon after on high-mileage engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuilt DV6 engine with refurbished injection system, full calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive solution; eliminates injector-related failures with warranty protection

problems.recommendation: If multiple injectors are faulty on a high-mileage Partner, a rebuilt engine with calibrated injection system offers better long-term value than piecemeal injector replacement.
problems.cta: Compare Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi rebuilt engine prices with refurbished injectors

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot Partner
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot Partner's commercial role means engine downtime directly impacts your business. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot Partner specialists who understand DPF, EGR and turbo failure patterns on high-mileage DV6 engines. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing known failure points, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty with nationwide delivery or supply & fit options. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot Partner Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot Partner and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot Partner Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Peugeot Partner across UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Peugeot Partner 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDi diesel (DV6 series) powers over 85% of UK Peugeot Partners, spanning Mk2 (2008-2018) and early Mk3 (2018+) generations. This efficient common-rail unit offers 75-110hp outputs and dominates commercial van use due to its balance of fuel economy and load-carrying capability.
commonFailurePoints: turbo oil seal leaks on high-mileage commercial use
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Partner 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Partner 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced on Mk3 Partners from 2019, the 1.5L BlueHDi (DV5RC) replaces the 1.6L unit with updated emissions technology and improved efficiency. This newer engine features revised belt-in-oil design and AdBlue/SCR systems for Euro 6d compliance.
commonFailurePoints: updated belt system requires specialist rebuild knowledge
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Partner 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Partner 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legacy 1.9L naturally aspirated diesel powered early Mk1 Partners (1996-2008). Simple, robust and easy to repair, these engines remain popular for budget-conscious commercial users despite lower power and higher emissions.
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Partner 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot Partner 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech three-cylinder petrol engine features on Mk3 Partners from 2018, offering a quieter, lower-emission alternative to diesel for light commercial and passenger Tepee use. Belt-in-oil design requires specialist attention during rebuilds.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Partner 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot Partner 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L VTi petrol engine appeared on Mk2 Partners (2009-2015), offering smooth performance for passenger-oriented Tepee models. Less common in commercial Panel Van configurations due to higher fuel costs versus diesel.
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Partner 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot Partner 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: Early Mk1 Partners (1996-2008) featured 1.4L petrol engines for entry-level commercial and passenger use. Simple SOHC design makes these affordable to rebuild but less powerful for laden van duties.
commonFailurePoints: oil consumption from piston ring wear
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Partner 1.4L petrol engine prices

engineSizes.closing: Not sure which engine size your Peugeot Partner needs? fuelTypes.h2: Peugeot Partner Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Peugeot Partner engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.9D workhorses to the latest BlueHDi and PureTech units.

items.title: Peugeot Partner Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates the Peugeot Partner range across all three generations, with the 1.6L HDi (DV6 series) powering over 85% of UK commercial vans due to its balance of economy, torque and reliability.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates the Peugeot Partner range across all three generations, with the 1.6L HDi (DV6 series) powering over 85% of UK commercial vans due to its balance of economy, torque and reliability.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot Partner Diesel Engine Families
families: DV6DTED - most common 1.6L HDi 75/90hp, high stock availability for commercial use
9HV/9HX - higher-output 1.6L HDi 90/110hp, turbo rebuild packages available for laden vans
DV6FC - 1.6L BlueHDi with AdBlue/SCR, updated emission calibration for 2018+ models
DV5RC - 1.5L BlueHDi successor, revised belt-in-oil design requires specialist rebuild knowledge
foundIn: Mk1 (1996-2008) - 1.9D naturally aspirated (WJY), simple and robust for budget commercial use
Mk2 (2008-2018) - 1.6L HDi DV6 series (DV6DTED, 9HV, 9HX), DPF-equipped from 2010
Mk3 (2018-2024) - 1.6L BlueHDi (DV6FC) and 1.5L BlueHDi (DV5RC), Euro 6d compliant
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy ideal for courier and tradesperson use
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey commercial patterns
EGR valve clogging and turbo wear on high-mileage units over 100k miles
High UK replacement demand driven by business downtime sensitivity
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot Partner Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Peugeot Partner Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Partners serve light commercial and passenger Tepee roles, with the modern 1.2L PureTech offering lower emissions and quieter operation versus diesel for urban use.
items.descriptor: Petrol Partners serve light commercial and passenger Tepee roles, with the modern 1.2L PureTech offering lower emissions and quieter operation versus diesel for urban use.
items.familiesLabel: Common Peugeot Partner Petrol Engine Families
families: EB2ADT - 1.2L PureTech 110hp, belt-in-oil design requires specialist rebuild attention
5FS - 1.6L VTi 120hp, timing chain tensioner wear common on higher-mileage units
HFX/KFU - 1.4L entry petrol, simple rebuilds but less suited to laden van duties
foundIn: Mk1 (1996-2008) - 1.4L petrol (HFX, KFU) for entry-level commercial/passenger use
Mk2 (2009-2015) - 1.6L VTi (5FS) primarily on Tepee passenger variants
Mk3 (2018-2024) - 1.2L PureTech (EB2ADT) on Active/Allure trims and Tepee MPV
knownFor: Quieter, smoother operation preferred for passenger Tepee use
Belt-in-oil degradation risk on 1.2L PureTech requires specialist rebuild knowledge
Lower torque versus diesel makes petrol less ideal for heavily laden commercial use
Growing replacement demand as early PureTech units reach high mileage

items.title: Peugeot Partner Electric / e-Partner

items.descriptor: The e-Partner electric van offers zero-emission urban commercial capability, with replacement focused on the electric drive unit and battery diagnostics rather than traditional engine rebuilds.

engineYears.h2: Peugeot Partner Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot Partner's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Partner established Peugeot's small van presence with simple, robust diesel and petrol engines ideal for budget-conscious tradespeople.
keyChanges: Launch of Peugeot Partner van/MPV dual-platform in UK market
Introduction of 1.9D naturally aspirated diesel for commercial durability
1.4L petrol options for entry-level passenger Tepee variants
Facelift in 2002 with updated interior and minor engine refinements
mainEngines: WJY - 1.9L diesel (69hp) Base, X, LX commercial variants
knownFor: Simple, robust engines easy for independent garages to repair
1.9D diesel renowned for longevity if serviced regularly
Low parts costs and wide aftermarket support for budget rebuilds
Injector seal leaks and glow plug issues common on high-mileage units
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2008 Peugeot Partner replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot Partner 1.9D engine replacement • Peugeot Partner Mk1 diesel price • Peugeot Partner 1.4L petrol rebuild cost

years.preview: Mk2 Launch: DV6 Diesel Dominance Begins
years.description: The all-new Mk2 Partner introduced the 1.6L HDi DV6 diesel family, transforming commercial capability with common-rail efficiency and DPF technology.
keyChanges: Complete platform redesign with improved safety and refinement
Introduction of 1.6L HDi DV6 diesel engines (DV6DTED, DV6TED4)
DPF-equipped variants from 2010 for Euro 5 compliance
New Professional trim targeting fleet and tradesperson markets
mainEngines: DV6DTED - 1.6L diesel (75/90hp) S, SE, Professional variants
knownFor: DV6DTED becomes UK's most searched Partner engine code for replacement
DPF regeneration issues emerge on short-journey commercial use patterns
Improved refinement and fuel economy versus Mk1, but more complex emissions systems
EGR valve clogging common on urban commercial delivery vehicles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2012 Peugeot Partner replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot Partner DV6DTED engine • Peugeot Partner DPF problem • Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi 90 replacement

years.preview: Mk2 Matures: DPF Standard, Power Options Expand
years.description: Mid-life Mk2 Partners standardise DPF across diesel range and introduce higher-output 110hp variants for laden commercial use.
keyChanges: DPF fitted to all 1.6L HDi diesel variants from 2013 onwards
Introduction of 9HX 110hp engine for Professional and Crew Van models
Updated EGR cooler specification to address clogging complaints
Tepee passenger variants gain 1.2L PureTech petrol option late in cycle
mainEngines: 9HX - 1.6L diesel (110hp) Professional, Crew Van, high-spec variants
knownFor: 9HV becomes highest-volume replacement engine code for commercial Partners
Turbo oil seal leaks emerge on 100k+ mile 9HX units used for heavy loads
DPF warning lights and forced regeneration cycles dominate failure enquiries
Belt-in-oil design on late PureTech units requires specialist rebuild knowledge
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2018 Peugeot Partner replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot Partner 9HV engine replacement • Peugeot Partner 110hp turbo failure • Peugeot Partner Tepee petrol engine

years.preview: Mk3 Launch: BlueHDi and PureTech Modernisation
years.description: The all-new Mk3 Partner introduces BlueHDi diesel with AdBlue/SCR and PureTech petrol with belt-in-oil design for Euro 6d compliance.
keyChanges: New EMP2 platform shared with Citroën Berlingo and Opel Combo
DV6FC 1.6L BlueHDi replaces DV6 series with AdBlue/SCR emission control
EB2ADT 1.2L PureTech becomes primary petrol option across range
e-Partner electric variant launched for zero-emission urban commercial use
mainEngines: e-Partner Motor - 82kW electric drive unit Panel Van
knownFor: AdBlue injector faults and SCR calibration issues dominate early BlueHDi enquiries
Belt-in-oil degradation risk on PureTech requires specialist rebuild expertise
Electric e-Partner replacement focuses on motor diagnostics, not traditional engine rebuilds
Cross-platform compatibility with Berlingo remains but ECU calibration differs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2021 Peugeot Partner replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot Partner BlueHDi AdBlue fault • Peugeot Partner PureTech belt issue • Peugeot Partner e-Partner motor repair

years.description: Latest Partners introduce the 1.5L DV5RC BlueHDi diesel successor alongside refined PureTech petrol for improved efficiency and emissions compliance.
keyChanges: DV5RC 1.5L BlueHDi replaces DV6FC with revised architecture and improved efficiency
Updated belt-in-oil specification on PureTech addresses early fragmentation concerns
Enhanced driver assistance and connectivity features across trim range
e-Partner battery capacity increased for extended urban commercial range
knownFor: DV5RC EGR cooler cracking on early production units requires updated component in rebuilds
Revised PureTech belt specification demands specialist inspection and rebuild expertise
Electric variant replacement remains specialist-domain with limited supplier availability
Commercial warranty terms and residual value critical for fleet replacement decisions
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022-2024 Peugeot Partner replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot Partner 1.5 BlueHDi EGR issue • Peugeot Partner PureTech updated belt • Peugeot Partner GT diesel replacement

faq.h2: Peugeot Partner Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot Partner engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Peugeot Partner engine replacement costs range from £795 for a verified used 1.6L HDi unit to £2,400+ for a reconditioned DV6 engine with warranty. Get instant quotes from commercial vehicle suppliers to compare options for your specific variant and engine code.
keyPoints: Used DV6DTED engines start from £795 supply-only
Reconditioned 9HV/9HX units typically £1,400-£1,900
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 at certified commercial garages
Next-day delivery available on stock engines to minimise business downtime
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Partner engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot Partner?
items.answer: Enter your engine code (DV6DTED, 9HV, 9HX, DV6FC) or registration for instant Berlingo-platform compatibility verification. Most UK Partners use 1.6L HDi DV6-series diesels with DPF from 2010 onwards.
keyPoints: DV6DTED: 75/90hp, most common commercial Partner engine code
9HV/9HX: 90/110hp, higher-output variants for laden vans
DV6FC: 1.6L BlueHDi with AdBlue for 2018+ Mk3 models
Cross-check with Citroën Berlingo but verify Peugeot-specific ECU calibration
items.cta: Use our free Peugeot Partner engine code lookup tool

items.question: Is a reconditioned Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes - specialist rebuilt DV6 engines with upgraded EGR/timing components and 12-month commercial warranty offer business peace of mind. Reconditioned units address known failure points for extended service life.
keyPoints: Rebuilds include new bearings, seals, gaskets and updated EGR cooler
Bench-tested with compression and leak-down verification before dispatch
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across EngineMarket network
Ideal for commercial users prioritising reliability over lowest upfront cost
items.cta: Get quotes for reconditioned Peugeot Partner DV6 engines with warranty

items.question: How long does Peugeot Partner engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines available on next-day delivery to minimise downtime. Supply & fit typically completed within 72 hours from order to collection at a certified commercial garage near you.
keyPoints: Stock DV6DTED/9HV engines dispatched same-day if ordered before 2pm
Supply & fit bookings scheduled at 100+ vetted UK commercial garages
Calibration and road test included in professional installation service
Express options available for urgent commercial vehicle requirements
items.cta: Book Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi supply & fit at a local commercial garage

items.question: Can I replace my Peugeot Partner engine myself?
items.answer: Not recommended - DV6 engines require specialist tools and ECU calibration. Use our commercial supply & fit network to maintain warranty validity and ensure correct DPF/EGR system integration.
keyPoints: DV6 timing belt replacement demands specialised locking tools
DPF/EGR calibration requires Peugeot-specific diagnostic equipment
Incorrect installation can void warranty and cause secondary failures
Certified commercial garages offer end-to-end service with warranty protection
items.cta: Find a certified Peugeot Partner engine fitting specialist near you

items.question: What causes Peugeot Partner 1.6 HDi engine failure?
items.answer: DPF regeneration issues and EGR clogging are primary causes on high-mileage commercial vans. Replacement engines include updated components addressing these failure points for improved reliability.
keyPoints: Short-journey commercial use prevents active DPF regeneration cycles
EGR valve carbon buildup restricts airflow and triggers fault codes
Turbo oil seal wear common on 100k+ mile units used for heavy loads
Rebuilt engines feature revised components to prevent recurrence
items.cta: Compare Peugeot Partner engines with updated DPF/EGR specifications

items.question: Do Peugeot Partner replacement engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes - 3/6/12-month commercial warranty options available, with nationwide garage support for warranty claims to keep your business running without extended downtime.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt/reconditioned units
Used engines typically include 3-month commercial warranty as standard
Warranty claims processed through local certified garages nationwide
Commercial fleet accounts may qualify for extended coverage options
items.cta: Upgrade to 12-month commercial warranty for Peugeot Partner engine peace of mind

items.question: How do I find my Peugeot Partner engine code?
items.answer: Check your V5 logbook, engine bay sticker, or use our free code lookup tool with registration for instant commercial compatibility verification and accurate quote matching.
keyPoints: V5C registration document lists engine code in section D.2
Engine code stamped on block near timing cover or oil filter housing
DV6DTED, 9HV, 9HX most common on UK commercial Partners
Enter registration above for instant verification and compatible engine options
items.cta: Verify your Peugeot Partner engine code instantly with our lookup tool

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Peugeot Partner engine?
items.answer: Often yes - replacement from £795 versus £20k+ for a new van. Get quotes to compare against vehicle residual value and your specific business needs before deciding.
keyPoints: Commercial Partners retain strong residual value if mechanically sound
Rebuilt engine cost typically 10-20% of new van purchase price
Minimise business downtime with next-day engine delivery options
Consider total cost of ownership versus replacement vehicle financing
items.cta: Get instant Peugeot Partner engine quotes to evaluate replacement value

items.question: Can I use a Citroën Berlingo engine in my Peugeot Partner?
items.answer: DV6 engines are shared with Berlingo, but Peugeot-specific ECU calibration is required. Use our compatibility checker to avoid costly fitment errors and warranty invalidation.
keyPoints: Physical engine fitment identical across Partner/Berlingo platform
ECU software, wiring harness and DPF calibration differ between brands
Incorrect calibration can cause limp mode, emission faults or warranty denial
EngineMarket specialists verify Peugeot-specific compatibility before supply
items.cta: Check Peugeot Partner Berlingo engine compatibility before ordering
